QF.id: Issue Management - General Obligations and Due Diligence_51

question.text: A company is making an IPO to raise Rs. 500 crore. The post-issue capital of the company will be Rs. 800 crore. What is the minimum promoter contribution required under SEBI ICDR Regulations?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For an IPO, the minimum promoter contribution is 20% of the post-issue capital. Here, 20% of Rs. 800 crore = Rs. 160 crore. The calculation is based on post-issue capital, not the issue size. **The Trap:** Rs. 100 crore (20% of Rs. 500 crore issue size) is tempting because candidates confuse issue size with post-issue capital. For IPOs, it is always post-issue capital that matters. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) and NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) also test knowledge of primary market regulations including promoter contribution norms. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always distinguish between IPO (20% of post-issue capital) and FPO (20% of proposed issue size) when calculating minimum promoter contribution.
